commands.review_summary:
The PR widens public skill-list cursor decoding to accept full matching getPage index keys for non-trending sorts, updates cursor pagination tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main emits getPage indexKeys as nextCursor but rejects replayed full keys that exceed the explicit index field count, and the linked report provides concrete curl steps for the public failure.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor-provided real API pagination proof and maintainer handling of the Vercel authorization status; there is no narrow automated source repair indicated.

target.title: fix(api): accept full skill list pagination cursors
target.body:
## Summary

Accepts the full public skill-list pagination cursor shape emitted by `getPage()` for non-trending public API sorts.

## What changed

- Self-describing public list cursors may include the full helper index key, including Convex tie-breaker fields.
- Legacy raw-array cursor validation remains strict.
- Pagination tests now mock realistic `getPage()` keys and cover first-page to second-page API pagination.

## Behavior proof

The regression test covers the user-visible failure mode: the first API page emits a full cursor, the next request accepts that cursor, and the API advances to the next page instead of restarting or treating the cursor as stale.

The public contract is unchanged: clients keep sending the cursor emitted by the previous response; the server now accepts the full cursor it already produced.

## Validation

```text
$ bun run test convex/skills.publicListCursor.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       5 passed (5)
```

```text
$ bun run ci:static
passed
```

```text
$ bun run ci:unit
Test Files  204 passed (204)
Tests       1986 passed (1986)
Statements  85.98%
Branches    75.17%
Functions   86.37%
Lines        89.48%
```

```text
$ bun run ci:types-build
tsc, package typechecks, clawhub-mod typecheck, Vite build, and Nitro build passed.
```

## External blocker

The known Vercel preview failure is team authorization required for deployment. That is maintainer-owned and not a patch failure.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the diff, can maintain it, and validated it with the commands above.
